cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes:
Overall performance is not far from Windows.
Just set shadows to "low" and enjoy 40 fps fluid 1080p or scale down a bit rendering option to reach 55-60 fps for proper online gaming. Had local pvp with two perfectly working steam controllers. Good :)
title: TEKKEN 7

notes: Upgraded my gpu from rx460 to rtx 2070, Game play is definitely better.But fame rates never above 60 dips to even 35 fps, With nvidia card having audio and video sync problem sometimes, which was not percent on amdgpu ..

notes:
Runs on Ultra graphics 1080p full screen, about 45-60 fps, keep shadows on low and vsync off. Xbox controller and arcade stick works great.
Some stages have frame drops, so to be on the safe side play around with your graphics settings.

notes: Runs perfectly ootb. Slightly faster loading times than with Proton 3.7-8 that Valve makes you use by default. You can safely use the latest with this one.

notes: the game doesn't deactivate DE compositing most of the times so you have to do it manually by changing the resolution one time inside the game or in your DE options or else you will get horrible frame-skipping and stuttering
title: TEKKEN 7

notes: Disable desktop compositor, play a few maps in practice mode to build up the shadercache. Stable 60 FPS (Game's locked to 60). No tweaks from that point on. No stutter no input-lag. Works fine with my fightstick Mayflash F500.
title: TEKKEN 7

notes: Doesn't launch fullscreen. KDE and Gnome3 panels visible. Need to right click game icon on panel and set to fullscreen. And as the others have mentioned, need to manually disable desktop compositing.

title: TEKKEN 7

notes: Game works perfectly out of the box with 32GB(4x8GB) RAM. With 16GB(2x8GB) the game crashes when trying to enter the main menu. I suspect the problem has something to do with running dual-channel memory on Threadripper, as with quad-channel the game works fine. 16GB should have been more than enough to launch the game. But I'm playing it safe and rating this silver for now, it is possible that this is not a Proton issue but rather something else like Kernel/Linux/CPU/Motherboard/BIOS etc..
title: TEKKEN 7

notes: No tweaks necessary. Works out of the box with a Hori Fighting Edge fight stick. Stable frame rate on all maps. No discernible increase in input latency as compared to Windows, meaning you can practice Tekken competitively on Linux.
title: TEKKEN 7

notes:
-EPILEPSY WARNING-
PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=0 %command%
Have to play @ 720p mostly minimum graphics to be able to stream it while playing but it's playable. If I don't run the launch option I've listed then all the character's skin colours becomes very tan even already tan/black characters get darker + epileptic cyan colors flash in the menus and loading screens.
title: TEKKEN 7

notes: Doesn't launch in fullscreen mode. You have to change the resolution to get the fullscreen working. The game messed up my controller buttons. Just set 'forced off' in Properties > Steam input to solve this. That's all.
title: TEKKEN 7

notes: Works perfectly out of the box, with Mesa ACO driver there is significantly less stuttering. Nintendo Switch Pro controller also worked. I rated platinum, but be aware that there may be an issue with running the game on Threadripper in dual-channel mode. I only tested with quad-channel this time.
title: TEKKEN 7

notes: Game works perfectly, with Mesa ACO there is much less stuttering than before.
